docs.title: API Reference
docs.servicesHint: Unknown service names will be recorded as "Other"
docs.adInfo: Optional fields for ad tracking (ip, url, account, campaign, ad group, ad). Viewable in lead details.
docs.bodyExample:
{
  "source": "website-form",
  "name": "Jane Smith",
  "contactInfo": "+1-555-0100",
  "intendedServices": ["IVF Abroad"],
  "notes": "notes (optional)",
  "adInfo": {
    "ip": "1.2.3.4",
    "url": "https://landing.example.com",
    "account": "Google Ads Account A",
    "campaign": "IVF-Global",
    "adGroup": "Women 25-35",
    "ad": "Creative Copy 01"
  }
}

intro.title: Workflow Automation
intro.subtitle: Understand how workflows run, trigger conditions, and execution flow
intro.body: Workflows automatically execute a series of actions when specific conditions are met, reducing manual work and ensuring consistent business processes. You can configure and enable workflows in System Settings → Workflows.

trigger.title: Trigger
trigger.desc: Entity created, or field changed to a specified value

match.title: Match
match.desc: Find all enabled workflows and check each trigger condition

execute.title: Execute
execute.desc: Run actions in order; earlier results are available to later template variables

onCreate.label: On Create
onCreate.desc: Fires immediately when an entity (lead/client) is created. Good for welcome notifications or initializing default field values.

fieldChange.label: Field Change
fieldChange.desc: Fires when a specified field changes to a particular value, e.g. Status → Confirmed, or Owner → Any. Supports 'Any' to fire on any change.

scheduled.label: Scheduled
scheduled.desc: Auto-scans all records daily at 09:00 (Asia/Shanghai) and executes actions on matching entities. Supports: date field = today, date field expired, no activity for N days.

intro.asyncDescPre: All workflow actions run asynchronously in the background (fire-and-forget) and do not block the frontend response.
intro.asyncDescPost: action's database writes happen in real time, but you need to refresh the page to see the updated values.

vars.title: Template Variables
vars.subtitle: Dynamically reference entity data and time in emails, webhooks, and field assignments
vars.unrecognizedPost: (not replaced with empty), making configuration errors easy to spot.
vars.timeNote: Time variables are calculated based on the timezone in system settings (default Asia/Shanghai). Week starts on Monday; month follows the calendar month.
vars.leadNotePost: come from the linked user table; other fields are read directly from the lead record.
vars.clientNotePost: come from the linked user table.
vars.clientNoteServicePlans: is an array; multiple items are joined with a comma.

actionsSection.title: Action Types
actionsSection.subtitle: Five actions available after a workflow triggers, with configuration guides

set_field.label: Auto Set Field
set_field.desc: Automatically set a field to a specified value when trigger conditions are met. Field values support template variables.
notes: Actions execute in list order; a previous action's result is immediately available to subsequent template variables
Field values can use template variables such as {{today}}, {{name}}, etc.

send_email.label: Send Email
send_email.desc: Send a notification email to specified recipients on trigger. Recipients, subject, and body all support template variables.
notes: Email is sent via SendGrid; configure SENDGRID_API_KEY in Cloudflare Workers environment variables
Recipient can be a fixed address or dynamic via {{assignedToEmail}} / {{assignedSalesEmail}}
Body is plain text; line breaks are preserved

webhook.label: Webhook
webhook.desc: Send an HTTP request to an external system on trigger. URL and body both support template variables.
notes: Body should be valid JSON; string values can embed {{variables}}
GET requests do not send a body

require_activity.label: Require Follow-up
require_activity.desc: Intercept user actions on the frontend and require a follow-up record before a status change can complete.
require_activity.example:
Trigger: Lead status → Lost
Require reason: Yes
Preset options: No interest, Price too high, Chose another provider
notes: Frontend only; backend API does not validate

require_fields.label: Require Fields
require_fields.desc: Require the user to fill in specified fields before completing an action; cannot proceed if fields are empty.
require_fields.example:
Trigger: Lead status → Confirmed
Required fields: Next Contact Date, Intended Services
notes: Frontend only; backend API does not validate

tipsSection.title: Notes
tipsSection.subtitle: Edge behaviors and security notes when using workflows

actionOrder.title: Action Execution Order
items: Actions within the same workflow run serially in list order
After set_field runs, the new value is immediately available to subsequent template variables
A failing action does not stop subsequent actions (each action has its own try-catch)

emailConfig.title: Email Configuration
items: Uses SendGrid for sending; configure SENDGRID_API_KEY in Cloudflare Workers environment variables
If SENDGRID_API_KEY is not set, send-email actions are silently skipped without error
Sender is fixed as noreply@irfc.cn (Fertility CRM)

unmatched.title: Behavior When Variables Don't Match
items: Using a non-existent variable name (e.g. {{xyz}}) keeps {{xyz}} as-is rather than replacing it with an empty string
This makes configuration errors easy to spot and avoids silently sending broken notifications
If a field value is null, the variable is not in the context and referencing it also keeps it as-is

timing.title: Trigger Timing & Frontend Refresh
items: Workflows run asynchronously in the background (fire-and-forget); frontend operations do not wait for completion
set_field writes to the database in real time, but you need to manually refresh the page to see the latest value
require_activity and require_fields only intercept on the frontend; backend API does not validate

webhookSecurity.title: Webhook Security
items: Webhook URLs support template variables; ensure URLs come from trusted sources to avoid SSRF risks
Request signing and HMAC verification are not yet supported; receivers should verify source IP or add a token parameter
Variable substitution in the body happens before sending; sensitive data (e.g. passwords) should not be passed via variables

overview.title: API Integration — Auth & Overview
overview.subtitle: Automatically import leads from external systems (landing pages, ad platforms, etc.) via REST API
overview.authDesc: All API requests must include an
overview.keyNote: API Keys are created and managed by admins in System Settings → API Keys. Keys start with
overview.keyNotePost: — keep them secret; never expose them in frontend code or public repositories.
overview.autoAssignDesc: Leads submitted via API automatically enter the assignment queue and are assigned to the right sales rep based on rules configured in System Settings → Assignment Rules.

errorCodes.400: Request validation failed; the message field explains the specific reason

leads.title: Submit Lead
leads.subtitle: Submit customer information collected from landing pages or ad platforms to the CRM
leads.adInfoNote: All adInfo sub-fields are optional. After submission they appear on the lead detail page in the Ad Info card.
fallbackItems: The system validates each item in intendedServices against the service list
Unrecognized service names are automatically replaced with 'Other' and the original name is appended to notes
If you submit an 'Other' service not configured in the system, first add it in System Settings → Service Management

fields.name: Lead name
fields.intendedServices: List of intended services, at least one. Unrecognized services fall back to 'Other' and are noted
fields.adInfo: Ad tracking info (optional); see ad fields below
